sanskrit: नान्यं गुणेभ्यः कर्तारं यदा द्रष्टानुपश्यति । गुणेभ्यश्च परं वेत्ति मद्भावं सोऽधिगच्छति ॥
hindiTranslation: जब विवेकी विचारकुशल मनुष्य तीनों गुणोंके सिवाय अन्य किसीको कर्ता नहीं देखता और अपनेको गुणोंसे पर अनुभव करता है, तब वह मेरे स्वरूपको प्राप्त हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: When the seer understands that no doer exists apart from the three qualities, and knows the divine nature beyond them, that seer reaches my being.
essence: Freedom begins when you stop mistaking qualities for a personal doer.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Clear seeing reveals that action belongs to the three qualities, not to a separate personal doer. When a person sees beyond them, they come to Krishna's nature.
simpleInsight: It brings relief from the burden of personal control.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna explains that the world runs through the three qualities, not through a separate ego-doer. The one who sees this clearly moves toward Krishna’s own nature.
modernRelevance: You replay a painful conversation for hours, blaming yourself for every word. Then it clicks: much of what happened came from moods, habits, and pressures larger than one isolated self.
translationLiteral: नान्यम् (no other) / गुणेभ्यः (than the qualities) / कर्तारम् (doer) / यदा (when) / द्रष्टा (the seer) / अनुपश्यति (sees clearly) / गुणेभ्यः (than the qualities) / च (and) / परम् (beyond) / वेत्ति (knows) / मद्भावम् (my nature) / सः (that one) / अधिगच्छति (attains)
